artifacts.title: Really receipt from 2026-05-04
artifacts.excerpt:
I was in a museum in Tashkent with a couple of good English blokes and mentioned that I'd gotten my phone snatched in London and that it was a shame because it really would be pretty easy to fix simply by arresting and incarcerating a few thousand criminals.  Their response:

"There aren't enough cops to do this."

Madness!  This is one of the easiest things to fix in criminal justice.  All that it lacking is a bit of will to enforce laws!

artifacts.title: Years receipt from 2026-05-24
artifacts.excerpt:
So this reminds me of a story.  Years ago, an emeritus professor in my department (I was a Ph.D. student) passed away and in his bequest he quite generously gave significant gifts to both the department and to faculty members he had served with.

As the provisions of the will were disclosed, there was a grant of funds large enough for a perpetual stipend for several research assistant positions, his world class collection of books and journals in the field were given new homes, and at the end "the entire store of my alcohol collection is for NAME, who I know will appreciate the gesture."

Now, this august professor had retired and so some faculty members had to go to his final home to collect the items, with great speculation and plans about the disposition of what would inevitably be a magnificent agglomeration of wine and spirits.
